question: The operation ⊗ is used to combine two nonzero numbers. Determine [(1 ⊗ 2) ⊗ 3] - [1 ⊗ (2 ⊗ 3)].

Doug constructs a square window using equal-size panes of glass. The ratio of the height to width for each pane is 5 : 2, and the borders around and between the panes are 2 inches wide. In inches, what is the side length of the square window?

Let P(x) be a polynomial of degree 3n such that
P(0) = P(3) = ... = P(3n) = 2,
P(1) = P(4) = ... = P(3(n-1)+1) = 1,
P(2) = P(5) = ... = P(3(n-2)+2) = 0.
Also, P(3n+1) = 730.
Determine n.

At Euclid Middle School the mathematics teachers are Mrs. Germain, Mr. Newton, and Mrs. Young. In Mrs. Germain's class there are more than ten but fewer than fifteen students taking the AMC 8, while Mr. Newton’s class has a small group of students that isn’t exactly defined, and Mrs. Young’s class has an odd number of participants that might be either 9 or 10. How many mathematics students at Euclid Middle School are taking the contest?
If 991 + 993 + p + 997 + 999 = 5000 - N, then N equals what, given that p is an unspecified number?

The total in-store price for an appliance is $99.99$. A television commercial advertises the same product for three easy payments of $29.98$ and a one-time shipping and handling charge. How many cents are saved by buying the appliance from the television advertiser?

Points A, B, C, D, E, and F lie, in that order, on a line, but the distances between consecutive points are not specified. Let G be some point not on this line. Choose points H on segment GD and J on segment GF. If the segments HC, JE, and AG are parallel, what is the ratio HC/JE?
During the softball season, Judy got several hits. Among her hits were 1 home run, 1 triple, and 5 doubles. The rest of her hits were singles. What percent of her hits were singles?
The graph, G, of y=log₁₀x is rotated counter-clockwise about the origin to obtain a new graph G'. What is the equation for G'?
A set of consecutive positive integers is written on a blackboard. One number is erased, and the average (arithmetic mean) of the remaining numbers is 35\frac{7}{17}. What number was erased?

A rectangular yard includes two flower beds shaped like triangles. The remaining part of the yard forms a shape that has one side of length 15 meters and another of length 25 meters, though their locations within the shape are not clearly defined. What fraction of the yard is covered by the flower beds?
What is the sum of all possible values of t such that the triangle in the coordinate plane whose vertices are (cos 40°, sin 40°), (cos 60°, sin 60°), and (cos t°, sin t°) is isosceles?

In the adjoining figure, points B and C lie on line segment AD, and AB, BC, and CD are diameters of circles O, N, and P, respectively. Circles O, N, and P all have radius 15 and the line AG is tangent to circle Q at G. If AG intersects circle N at points E and F, then chord EF has length
The first three terms of a sequence are x - 1, x + 1, 2x + 3. What is the value of x?

Alicia had two containers. The first container initially held some water and the second container was empty. After she poured the water from the first container into the second container, the water level in the second container was described in a vague way. What is the ratio of the volume of the first container to the volume of the second container?

An architect is building a structure that places vertical pillars at the vertices of a hexagon labeled ABCDEF, though the exact shape and orientation of the hexagon relative to the ground are not fully defined. The six pillars support a flat solar panel whose tilt and relation to the ground are also not specified. The heights of the pillars at A, B, and C are given as 12, 9, and 10 meters, respectively. What is the height of the pillar at E?
The points (2,-3), (4, ?), and (5, k/2) are on the same straight line. What is the value of k?

In a certain year the price of gasoline rose by 20% during January, fell by 20% during February, rose during March, and fell by x% during April. The price of gasoline at the end of April was the same as it had been at the beginning of January. To the nearest integer, what is x?

Quadrilateral ABCD satisfies \(\angle ABC = \angle ACD = 90^{\circ}\) and \(AC=20.\) Diagonals \(\overline{AC}\) and \(\overline{BD}\) intersect at point \(E,\) and \(AE=5.\) Given that side \(CD\) is part of the quadrilateral, what is the area of quadrilateral \(ABCD\)?
The angle bisector of the acute angle at the origin formed by the graphs of the lines y = x and a certain line has equation y = kx. What is k?
A set S of points in the xy-plane obeys certain symmetry properties. Given that (2,3) is one of its points, what is the smallest possible number of points in S?
Five positive consecutive integers starting with a have average b. What is?

At noon on a certain day, Minneapolis is N degrees warmer than St. Louis. Some hours later, Minneapolis’s temperature decreases and St. Louis’s temperature increases by unspecified amounts so that the temperature difference between the two cities becomes 2 degrees. What is the product of all possible values of N?

Consider all 1000-element subsets of the set of all positive integers. From each such subset, choose the smallest element. The arithmetic mean of all these smallest elements is expressed in lowest terms as p/q, with p and q relatively prime positive integers. Find p + q.

A man on his way to dinner observes that the hands of his watch form an angle of 110°. Sometime later, he again notices that his watch shows an angle of 110°. Assuming everything happens normally, how many minutes did he spend away?
A 3x3x3 cube is made of 27 dice. What is the smallest possible sum of all of the numbers visible on its 6 faces?

In the multiplication problem below, A, B, C, and D are different digits. The three‐digit number ABA is multiplied by the two‐digit number CD to produce a four‐digit number written as C ? C D, where the second digit of the product is not clearly defined. What is A+B?

Andrea and Lauren are roughly 20 kilometers apart. They bike toward one another, with Andrea going much faster than Lauren, and the distance between them decreasing—though the rate isn’t clearly stated. At some point, Andrea stops biking due to a flat tire and waits. When Lauren reaches her, how many minutes have elapsed since they began biking?
The sum of two natural numbers is 17402. One of these numbers is divisible by 10, and if you remove one digit from it, you obtain the other number. What is the difference between the two numbers?
What is the value of $2^{0^{1^y}} + (2^0)^{1^z}$?

If a and b are digits for which

     2 a
  ×  b 3
  ---------
    6 9
  ? ?
  ---------
9 8 ?

then a+b =
If x, 2x+2, 3x+3, ... are in geometric progression with the common ratio equal to the number of apples in an orchard, what is the fourth term?
At 2:15 o'clock, the hour and minute hands of a clock form an angle that is three times the angle measured during its last calibration. What is the current angle?

Square EFGH has one vertex on each side of square ABCD. Point E is on side AB, and the distance from A to E is somewhat more than 7 times the distance from E to B. What is the ratio of the area of square EFGH to that of square ABCD?

Chandler wants to buy a mountain bike. For his birthday, he receives money from several family members and earns money weekly from his paper route. If he uses all the birthday money and his weekly earnings to buy the bike, in how many weeks will he be able to purchase it?
The harmonic mean of a set of non-zero numbers is the reciprocal of the average of the reciprocals of the numbers. What is the harmonic mean?
Julie is preparing a speech for her class. The ideal rate of speech is 150 words per minute. If Julie speaks at this rate, how many words should her speech contain?

Let \(\triangle ABC\) be an equilateral triangle. Extend side \(\overline{AB}\) beyond B to a point B' such that \(BB'\) is more than twice but less than four times the length of \(AB\). Similarly, extend side \(\overline{BC}\) beyond C to a point C' so that \(CC'\) is more than twice but less than four times the length of \(BC\), and extend side \(\overline{CA}\) beyond A to a point A' so that \(AA'\) is more than twice but less than four times the length of \(CA\). What is the ratio of the area of \(\triangle A'B'C'\) to the area of \(\triangle ABC\)?

If the following instructions are carried out by a computer, what value of \(X\) will be printed because of instruction \(5\)?

1. START \(X\) at a certain number and \(S\) at an initial value.  
2. Increase the value of \(X\) by 2.  
3. Increase the value of \(S\) by the current value of \(X\).  
4. If \(S\) is at least a particular limit, then go to instruction \(5\); otherwise, go back to instruction \(2\).  
5. Print the value of \(X\).  
6. Stop.
Letters A, B, C, and D represent four different digits. If (A+B)/(C+D) is an integer that is as large as possible, what is the value of A+B?

A shopper plans to purchase an item that may have any listed price and can use one of three coupons. Coupon A gives 15% off the listed price, Coupon B gives $30 off the listed price, and Coupon C gives 25% off the amount by which the listed price exceeds (blank). Let x and y be the smallest and largest prices, respectively, for which Coupon A saves at least as many dollars as either Coupon B or Coupon C. What is y - x?
In triangle ABC, ∠ABC = 45°. A point D is chosen on side BC such that ∠DAB = 15°. Find ∠ACB.

Mary's top book shelf holds five books with the following widths, in centimeters: 6, 1/2, 1, 2.5, and 10. If one of the books is removed, what is the average width of the remaining books in centimeters?
Assume x is a complex number. The sum of the greatest integer less than or equal to x and the least integer greater than or equal to x is 5. Determine the solution set for x.

A student council must select a two-person welcoming committee and a planning committee from among its members. There are exactly $10$ ways to select the two-person team. Given that students are allowed to serve on both groups, in how many different ways can the planning committee be chosen?

Some time ago, Pete was three times as old as his cousin Claire. At an even earlier, unspecified moment, Pete was four times as old as Claire. In how many years from now will the ratio of their ages be 2 : 1?

A square piece of paper is folded in half vertically. Both layers are then cut into two parts in a direction parallel to the fold, but the exact location of the cut is not fixed. Three new rectangles are formed—a large one and two small ones. What is the ratio of the perimeter of one of the small rectangles to the perimeter of the large rectangle?
Positive integers a and b are such that the graphs of y = ax + 5 and y = 3x + b intersect at a point. What is the sum of all possible x-coordinates of these points of intersection?

Four circles, no two of which are congruent, have centers at A, B, C, and D, and points P and Q lie on all four circles. The radius of circle A is 5/8 times the radius of circle B. Furthermore, AB = 39 and PQ = 48. Let R be the midpoint of PQ. What is AR+BR+CR+DR?
Medians AD and EF of \(\triangle ABC\) intersect in M. The midpoint of AE is N. Let the area of \(\triangle MNE\) be k times the area of \(\triangle ABC\). Then k equals:
Find the minimum value of \(\sqrt{x^2+y^2}\) if \(5x+12y = c\).
On average, for every 4 sports cars sold at the local dealership, sedans are sold. The dealership predicts that it will sell 28 sports cars next month. How many sedans does it expect to sell?

Two fair dice, each with at least 6 faces, are rolled. The probability of rolling a sum of 7 is ¾ of the probability of rolling a sum of 10, and the probability of rolling a sum of 12 is 1/12. What is the least possible number of faces on the two dice combined?

Two long cylindrical tubes of the same length but different diameters lie side by side on a flat surface. The larger tube, with radius 72, rolls along the surface toward the smaller tube, which has radius 24; it rolls over the smaller tube and continues on until it comes to rest on a point of its circumference that is identical to its starting point after one complete self-revolution. Without specifying whether the contact forces guarantee perfect rolling or if external factors affect the motion, and with the smaller tube’s response not clearly defined, express the distance x that the larger tube travels in the form aπ + b√c (with a, b, and c integers and c square‐free) and then determine a+b+c.
A certain number can be written as a fraction. When reduced to lowest terms, what is the sum of the numerator and denominator of this fraction?
For all non-zero numbers x and y, \(\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)\left(y+\frac{1}{y}\right)\) equals what value?
Consider the quadratic equation 2x^2 - kx + x + 8 = 0, which is known to sometimes exhibit a particular behavior. For which values of k does this behavior occur?
How many perfect cubes lie between 2^8+1 and 2^n+1, inclusive?
A line that passes through the origin intersects both the line x = 1 and the line y = 1+ \frac{\sqrt{3}}{3} x. What is the perimeter of the triangle formed by these three lines?
Alicia earns 20 dollars per hour, of which a certain percentage is deducted for local taxes. How many cents per hour of Alicia's wages go towards local taxes?
How many positive factors of 36 are also multiples of?

The circumference of a circle with center O is divided into 12 arcs, labeled with the letters A through L. If angle x is formed by joining two consecutive points and angle y is determined by drawing a line from an unspecified point on the circle to a point that is not among points A through L, what is the sum of angles x and y in degrees?
Mary thought of a positive two-digit number. She multiplied it by 3 and added 11. Then she switched the digits of the result, obtaining a new number. What was Mary's number?

Trapezoid ABCD has \(\overline{AB} \parallel \overline{CD}\) and \(BC = CD\), and it is given that \(\overline{AD}\) is perpendicular to \(\overline{BD}\). Let \(O\) be the intersection of the diagonals \(\overline{AC}\) and \(\overline{BD}\), and let \(P\) be the midpoint of \(\overline{BD}\). If \(OP=11\), the length of \(AD\) can be expressed in the form \(m\sqrt{n}\) (with \(m\) and \(n\) positive integers and \(n\) squarefree). What is \(m+n\)?

If q₁(x) and r₁ are the quotient and remainder, respectively, when the polynomial x⁸ is divided by x + 1/2, and if q₂(x) and r₂ are the quotient and remainder, respectively, when q₁(x) is divided by ax + b, then r₂ equals?
Determine the value of the expression \(\frac{1^{4y-1}}{5^{-n}+3^{-1}}\) given that y and n are distinct positive integers with n strictly greater than y.

Carrie has a rectangular garden that measures 6 feet by 8 feet. She plants part of the garden with strawberry plants in a pattern that can vary, and the amount of strawberries each plant produces changes from day to day. How many strawberries can she expect to harvest?
The sides of a triangle have lengths 6.5, 10, and s. What is the smallest possible value of s?

In a cube with vertices labeled in some order, let one cross-sectional area be defined by four points chosen along the edges (one point on an edge between two unspecified vertices, another on a different edge, and two more chosen arbitrarily on edges) so that the resulting plane cuts through the cube. If R is the ratio of this cross-sectional area to one face's area, what is R^2?
A square and an equilateral triangle have perimeters. Let A be the area of the circle circumscribed about the square and B the area of the circle circumscribed around the triangle. Find A/B.
Quadrilateral ABCD has AB = BC = CD and m∠BCD = 170°. What is the degree measure of ∠BAD?
Let (a_n) and (b_n) be sequences of real numbers defined by the equation \( (2 + i)^n = a_n + b_n i \), where i = \(\sqrt{-1}\). What is the value of \(\sum (a_n b_n)/7^n\)?
If each of two intersecting lines intersects a hyperbola, then the possible number of points of intersection with the hyperbola is:
The Fort Worth Zoo has a variety of two-legged birds and four-legged mammals. On one visit to the zoo, Margie counted 522 legs. How many of the animals that Margie counted were two-legged birds?
If \(\frac{1}{x} - \frac{1}{y} = \frac{1}{w}\), then \(z\) equals:

A rectangular floor measures a by b feet, where a and b are positive integers with b > a. An artist paints a rectangle on the floor with the sides of the rectangle parallel to the floor’s sides. The unpainted portion forms a border around the painted rectangle, and this border takes up half of the entire floor’s area. How many possibilities are there for the ordered pair (a, b)?
A palindrome between $1000$ and $10000$ is chosen at random. What?
What is the greatest three-digit positive integer n for which the sum of the first n positive integers is not a divisor?
In triangle ABC, altitude CH and median CM are drawn so that they trisect one of the angles. If the area of triangle CHM is K, what is the area of triangle ABC?

Claire adds the degree measures of the interior angles of a convex polygon and gets a certain total. Afterward she finds that one angle was not included in her calculation. What is the degree measure of the forgotten angle?

Zara has a collection of 4 marbles: an Aggie, a Bumblebee, a Steelie, and a Tiger. She wants to display them in a row on a shelf, but she doesn’t want to place two specific marbles next to each other. In how many ways can she arrange her marbles?
Triangle ABC has vertices A = (3,0), B = (0,3), and C. What is the area of triangle ABC?
Everyday at school, Jo climbs a flight of stairs. Jo can take the stairs 1, 2, or 3 at a time. For example, Jo could climb 3, then 1, then 2. In how many ways can Jo climb the stairs?
A team won 40 of its first 50 games. How many of its remaining games must it win for the season to end with exactly a 70% winning percentage?

In this diagram, not drawn to scale, Figures I and III are equilateral triangular regions with respective areas of 32√3 and 8√3 square inches. Figure II is a square region. If the length of segment AD is decreased by a modest percentage of its original length while the lengths of segments AB and CD remain unchanged, what is the percent decrease in the area of the square?
Positive integers a and b are given, where a is less than 6. What is the smallest possible value for 2 · a - a · b?
In square ABCD, points P and Q lie on sides AD and AB respectively. Segments BP and CQ intersect at right angles at R, with PR = 7. What is the area of the square?

The equations of L1 and L2 are y = mx and y = nx, respectively. Suppose L1 makes an angle with a line that is determined by an unspecified reference compared to L2, and that L1 has 4 times the slope of L2. What is the value of mn?

An amusement park has a collection of scale models of buildings and other attractions from around the country. The United States Capitol is 289 feet tall. What is the height in feet of its duplicate to the nearest whole number?
Sides \(\overline{AB}\) and \(\overline{AC}\) of triangle \(ABC\) are tangent to a circle at some points on those sides. What fraction of the area of \(\triangle ABC\) lies outside the circle?

Let \(x_n\) be defined by the rule
\[
x_n = \begin{cases} 2x_{n-1} & \text{ if } 2x_{n-1} < 1,\\ 2x_{n-1} - 1 & \text{ if } 2x_{n-1} \ge 1,\
\end{cases}
\]for every integer n > 0. For how many initial values x\(_0\) does the equality \(x_0 = x_5\) hold?

Logan is constructing a scaled model of his town. The city's water tower stands 40 meters high and its top is a sphere. Logan's miniature water tower holds 0.1 liters. How tall, in meters, should Logan make his tower?

In ΔPQR with side lengths 15, 20, and 25, points A and B lie on \(\overline{PQ}\), points C and D lie on \(\overline{QR}\), and points E and F lie on \(\overline{PR}\), with the segments \(PA, QB, QC, RD, RE, PF\) all equal. Find the area of hexagon ABCDEF.
Adams plans a profit on the selling price of an article and his expenses are 15% of sales. The rate of markup on an article that sells for $5.00 is:
What is the tens digit of 7 raised to the power of a number?

For a set of four distinct lines in a plane, there are exactly N distinct points that lie on two or more of the lines, under the additional condition that each of these points is also the center of a circle whose radius is in direct proportion to the measure of the angle between the intersecting lines (with no further specification provided for the constant of proportionality). What is the sum of all possible values of N?
Let f(t)=\frac{t}{1-t}, t \not= 1.  If y=f(x), then
For every dollar Ben spent on bagels, David spent $25$ cents less. How much did they spend in the bagel store together?
If $x \geq 0$, then $\sqrt{x\sqrt{x\sqrt{xy}}} =$
The complex number z satisfies z + |z| = 2 + ti, where t is a positive number. What is |z|^{2}?
There are seats in a row. What is the fewest number of seats that must be occupied so that when someone sits down next, they have to sit next to another person?
What is $10 \cdot \left(\frac{1}{2} + \frac{1}{x} + \frac{1}{10}\right)^{-1}$?
Jose is 4 years younger than Zack. Zack is 3 years older than Inez. How old is Jose?
The sum of all the roots of the equation is:

Nonzero real numbers x, y, a, and b satisfy x < a. How many of the following inequalities must be true?
(I)  x+y < a+b
(II) x-y < a-b
(III) xy < ab
(IV) x/y < a/b

After finding the average of 35 scores, a student carelessly included the median of the 35 scores with the 35 scores and computed the average of these 36 numbers. What is the ratio of this new average to the true average?

A gallon of paint is used to paint a room. On the first day, some of the paint is used, and on the second day, one third of the remaining paint is used. What fraction of the original amount of paint is available to use on the third day?
What is the value of the expression \(\sqrt{16\sqrt{8\sqrt{x}}}\) ?

A jacket and a shirt originally sold for $80 and $40, respectively, were purchased during a sale where the discounts on each item varied. Chris saved a certain amount on each item during the sale. What percent of the combined original prices did Chris save?

Penniless Pete's piggy bank has no pennies in it, but it has coins, all nickels, dimes, and quarters, whose total value is $8.35. It does not necessarily contain coins of all three types. What is the difference between the largest and smallest number of dimes that could be in the bank?
Six distinct positive integers are randomly chosen. What is the probability that some pair of these integers has a difference that is a multiple of 5?

Keiko walks once around a track at exactly the same constant speed every day. The sides of the track are straight, and the ends are semicircles. It takes her 36 seconds longer to walk around the outside edge of the track than around the inside edge. What is Keiko's speed in meters per second?

Semicircles POQ and ROS are drawn from two arbitrarily chosen chords of circle O such that each semicircle passes through the center O. What is the ratio of the combined areas of these two semicircles to the area of circle O?
An automobile travels a/6 feet in r seconds. If this rate is maintained for a certain period, how many yards does it travel?
The ratio of the radii of two concentric circles is 1:3. If AC is a diameter of the larger circle and BC is a chord of the larger circle, and AB = 12, what is the radius of the larger circle?
For real numbers a and b, consider an operation denoted by a diamond b. What is the value of (5 diamond 12) diamond ((-12) diamond (-5))?
Consider the expression (20! - ?!). What is the hundreds digit of this expression?
How many positive integer factors of $2020$ have more factors than a given number?

Every week Roger pays for a movie ticket and a soda out of his allowance. Last week, Roger's allowance was $A$ dollars. His movie ticket cost was 20% of the difference between $A$ and the cost of his soda, while his soda cost was of the difference between $A$ and the cost of his movie ticket. To the nearest whole percent, what fraction of $A$ did Roger pay?

Before the district play, the Unicorns had won 45% of their basketball games. During district play, they played additional games that improved their record, and by the end of the season they won half of the games they played. How many games did the Unicorns play in all?
Oscar buys some pencils and a few erasers for $1.00$. A pencil costs more than an eraser, and both items cost a whole number of cents. What is the total cost, in cents, of one pencil and one eraser?

Points B and C are located somewhere along the infinite line that contains segment AD, but their order relative to points A and D is not specified. The length of AB is 4 times the length of BD, and the length of AC is 9 times the length of CD. What fraction of the distance between A and D is BC?

A school has 100 students and 5 teachers. In the first period, every student is enrolled in exactly one class, and each teacher teaches one class. The enrollments in four of these classes are given as 50, 20, 20, and 5 students, while the enrollment for the remaining class is not provided. Let t be the average number of students in a class when a teacher is chosen at random, and let s be the average number of students noted by picking a student at random (the count in that student’s class, including the student). What is t-s?
What is the greatest number of consecutive integers?
What is the greatest possible sum of the digits in the base-seven representation of a positive integer?
A pair of standard 6-sided dice is rolled once. What is the probability that the numerical value of the area of a circle is less than the numerical value of its circumference?
The measures of the interior angles of a convex polygon of n sides are in arithmetic progression. If the largest angle is 160°, then n equals:
The fraction \(\frac{a^{-4}-b^{-4}}{a^{-2}-b^{-2}}\) is equal to the number of trees planted in the orchard. How many trees are there?
A triangle and a trapezoid are equal in area. If the base of the triangle is 18 inches, what is the median of the trapezoid?

A closed box with a square base is to be wrapped using a square sheet of paper. The box is positioned on the paper such that its base is not aligned with any specific markers on the paper, and the corners of the paper are folded up so that they meet at an unspecified point on the top of the box. If the box has base length w and height h, what is the area of the sheet of paper used?
Of the following expressions the one equal to \(\frac{a^{-1}c^{-1}}{a^{-3} - b^{-3}}\) is:
What is the value of \(\frac{11! - 10!}{ }\)?
Let A, M, and C be digits with (100A+10M+C)(A+M+C) = 2005. What is B?
Let f be a function for which f(x/3) = x^2 + x + 1. Find the sum of all values of z for which f(3z) = ?.

Frieda the frog begins a sequence of hops on a grid of squares, moving one square on each hop and choosing at random one of the four directions (up, down, left, or right). When a hop would take her off the grid, she wraps around to the opposite edge. Starting from the center square, if she makes up to four hops and stops as soon as she lands on a marked square, what is the probability that one of her hops ends on a marked square?
If f(a)=a-2 and F(a,b)=b^2+a, then what is F(3, f(n))?
What is the number of digits in $4^a5^b$ (when written in the usual base $10$ form) if a and b are unspecified positive integers?
How many integers n ≥ 2 are there?
If x cows give x+1 cans of milk and x+3 cows give x+5 cans of milk, how many days did it take for the larger group to produce that milk?
The set {3,6,9,10} is augmented by a fifth element n. What is the sum of all possible values of n?

A 6-inch and an 18-inch diameter pole are arranged in a way that their positions relative to each other are not fixed, and they are bound with a wire that is meant to wrap around them. What is the minimum length of wire required to encircle the poles?
Six rectangles have lengths of 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, and 36. What is the sum of the areas of these rectangles?

650 students were surveyed about their pasta preferences, choosing among four types of pasta. The results are shown in a bar graph that has unlabeled and unevenly scaled bars, making it unclear which bars correspond to which pasta type. What is the ratio of the number of students who preferred spaghetti to the number of students who preferred manicotti?

In counting n colored balls, some red and some black, it was noted that most of the first 50 balls were red. Thereafter, in every set of 8 balls, more than half were red. If overall the red balls comprise a prevailing majority of the total counted, what is the maximum possible n?

A figure can be folded along some lines to form a cube. At each corner of this cube, three faces meet. If the face values might differ when the figure is folded, what is the largest possible sum of the three numbers that come together at one corner?

Many calculators have a reciprocal key \(\boxed{1/x}\) that not only replaces the current number with its reciprocal but also mysteriously alters the displayed digits by an unseen formatting process. For example, if the display is \(\boxed{00004}\) and you press the \(\boxed{1/x}\) key, the result is shown as \(\boxed{000.25}\) under some unknown rules. If the display shows \(\boxed{00032}\) at one moment, what is the fewest number of times you must depress the \(\boxed{1/x}\) key so that, after these undetermined formatting quirks, the display exactly reads \(\boxed{00032}\) again?
The digits 1, 2, 3, 4 and 9 are each used once to form a five-digit number. The digit in the tens place is

In a magic triangle, each of the six whole numbers 10–15 is placed in one of the circles. The sum S is obtained by adding any three numbers chosen from circles that need not lie consecutively on a single side of the triangle. What is the largest possible value for S?
Suppose that x and y are nonzero real numbers such that (3x+y)/(x-3y) = -2 and x > 3y. What is the value of (x+3y)/(3x-y)?
In a numeral system where the base is set by the number directly preceding .125, compute the value of the product 8 × .25 × 2 × .125.

In parallelogram ABCD, \(\overline{DE}\) is drawn perpendicular from D to side AB and \(\overline{DF}\) is drawn perpendicular from D to side BC. [Note: Both figures represent the same shape.] Suppose that side DC measures 12 and DE is 6. Also, imagine that the distance from the foot of the perpendicular on AB to B is not fixed at 4 but is described only as "close to 4"—how does this affect the length of DF?
Four congruent rectangles are placed as shown. What is the ratio of the length of the longer side of each rectangle to the length of its shorter side?

Alice refuses to sit next to either Bob or Carla except when Carla is immediately between Bob and someone else, and Derek refuses to sit next to Eric unless Bob is sitting next to Eric. How many ways are there for the five individuals to arrange themselves in a row of chairs under these conditions?

If rectangle ABCD has area 72 square meters and E and G are points on sides AD and CD, respectively, where E can be anywhere on AD and G can be anywhere on CD, then what is the area of rectangle DEFG in square meters?
If $P_1P_2P_3P_4P_5P_6$ is a regular hexagon and $Q_i$ is the midpoint of side $P_iP_{i+1}$ for $i=1,2,3$, then what is the area of quadrilateral $Q_1Q_2Q_3Q_4$?

Alice, Bob, and Carol play a game in which each of them chooses a real number between 0 and 1. The winner of the game is the one whose number falls between the numbers chosen by the other two players. Alice announces that she will choose her number uniformly at random from 0 to 1, and Bob announces that he will choose his number uniformly at random from some subinterval of the interval from 0 to 1. With only this information, what number should Carol choose to maximize her chance of winning?
The sum of the real values of x satisfying the equality |x + ?| = 2|x - ?| is:
Let ABCD be an isosceles trapezoid with BC ∥ AD and AB = CD. Points X and Y lie on diagonal AC with X between A and Y. Suppose ∠AXD = ∠BYC = 90°, AX = 3, and XY = 1. What is the area of ABCD?
A cylindrical tank with a radius of 4 feet is lying on its side. The tank is filled with water to a depth of 2 feet. What is the volume of water, in cubic feet?
The number of revolutions of a wheel, whose size is given imprecisely, required to cause a point on its rim to travel a distance of a mile is:
Compute $\frac{(0.2)^k}{(0.02)^2}$, where k is a positive integer.

Each vertex of a cube is to be labeled with an integer 1 through 8, with each integer being used once, in such a way that the sum of the four numbers on the vertices of a face is the same for each face. Arrangements that can be obtained from each other through rotations of the cube are considered to be the same. How?

Six ants are positioned, one per vertex, on a polyhedron. At the same time, each ant independently moves along one of the connections from its vertex, but the number of connections available is not specified. What is the probability that no two ants end up at the same vertex?
Points P and Q are on line segment AB, and both points are on the same side of the midpoint of AB. Point P divides AB in the ratio 2:3, and Q divides AB. If PQ=2, then the length of segment AB is
If f(x)=\frac{x^4+x^2}{x+1}, then what is the value of f(j)?

Joy has 30 thin rods, one each of every integer length from 1 cm through 30 cm. She places three of these rods on a table. She then wants to choose a fourth rod from the remaining rods so that, when combined with the three already on the table, they form a quadrilateral with positive area. How many of the remaining rods can she choose as the fourth rod?

Malcolm wants to visit Isabella after school today and knows the street where she lives but doesn't know her house number. She tells him, "My house number has two digits, and several of the following four statements about it are true." (1) It is prime. (2) It is even. (3) It is divisible by 7. (4) One of its digits is 9. This information allows Malcolm to determine Isabella's house number. What is its units digit?

Samantha lives several blocks away from a corner of City Park and her school is a number of blocks away from another corner of the park. On school days she bikes along city streets to reach one corner of the park, then heads diagonally across the park, and finally rides on streets to her school. If her route is as short as possible, how many different routes can she take?

Alice needs to replace a light bulb located somewhere below the ceiling in her kitchen. The ceiling is above the floor, and Alice is 1.5 meters tall and can reach somewhat above her head when standing on a stool. If she barely manages to touch the light bulb, what is the height of the stool, in centimeters?

The manager of a company planned to distribute a bonus to each employee from the company fund, but the money available wasn’t quite enough for that plan. Instead, the manager gave each employee a smaller bonus and kept an unspecified remainder in the company fund. What was the amount of money in the company fund before any bonuses were paid?
Carl has several cubes and Kate has some cubes, with the side lengths of the cubes varying in an unspecified manner. What is the total volume of all the cubes?

Joe had walked part of the way from home to school when he realized he was late. He then ran the remaining distance without any indication of his running speed relative to his walking. Joe took 6 minutes to complete his walk. How many minutes did it take Joe to get from home to school?
What is the value of \(\frac{(2112- )^2}{169}\)?

A game is played with tokens according to the following rule. In each round, the player with the most tokens gives one token to each of the other players and also places one token in the discard pile. The game ends when some player runs out of tokens. Players A, B, and C start with 15, 14, and tokens, respectively. How many rounds will there be in the game?
Circle C1 has its center O lying on circle C2. The two circles meet at X and Y. A point Z outside C1 lies on C2, and it is given that XZ = 13 and YZ = 7. What is the radius of circle C1?
Rectangle ABCD has AB = 4 and BC = 3. A segment is drawn through B such that it is perpendicular to BD. What is the length of this segment?
Consider the set of numbers {1, 10, 10^2, 10^3, …}. The ratio of the largest element of this set to the sum of the remaining elements is closest to which integer?
The five tires of a car were rotated so that each tire was used equally during the car's journey. For how many miles was each tire used?
In rectangle ABCD, AB is 6 and AD is 8. Point M is a point on side AD. What is the area of triangle AMC?

If f(x)=3x+2 for all real x, then the statement:
"|f(x)+m| < a whenever |x+2| < b and a > 0" 
is true when?
Points A and B are 5 units apart in a plane. For a fixed number d, how many lines in that plane are exactly 2 units from A and d units from B?
A bug crawls along a number line. It crawls to -6, then turns around and crawls to 5. How many units does the bug crawl altogether?

Arjun and Beth play a game in which they take turns removing one brick or two adjacent bricks from one "wall" among a collection of several walls of bricks. Although the walls are said to be one brick tall, in this version the actual arrangement of bricks depends on a secret rule that isn’t revealed. For instance, a set of walls resembling the sizes 4 and 2 might change into various forms after one move, but the exact possibilities are left unspecified. Arjun plays first and the game ends when the last brick is removed. With this hidden detail in mind, for which starting configuration is there a strategy that guarantees a win for Beth?

In a collection of red, blue, and green marbles, there are a bit more red marbles than blue ones and a slightly higher number of green marbles than red ones. Assume there are r red marbles. What is the total number of marbles in the collection?
If an integer of two digits is a multiple of the sum of its digits, the number formed by interchanging its digits is the sum of its digits multiplied by what number?
If F(n+1)=\frac{2F(n)+1}{2} for n=1,2,\cdots, then what is F(101)?
A red ball and a green ball are randomly and independently tossed into bins. What is the probability that the red ball ends up in a higher-numbered bin than the green ball?
Granny Smith has $63. Elberta has $2 more than Anjou and Anjou has a fractional amount of Granny Smith's money. How many dollars does Elberta have?

In the figure, the outer equilateral triangle has area 16 and the inner equilateral triangle’s measurement is given in a way that leaves its exact value uncertain, while the three trapezoids are stated to be congruent. What is the area of one of these trapezoids?
A number x is 2 more than the product of its reciprocal. In which interval does the number lie?
A rectangle is inscribed in a semicircle with one of its sides lying on the diameter. What is the area of the semicircle?
Patty has coins consisting of nickels and dimes. If her nickels were dimes and her dimes were nickels, she would have 70 cents more. How much are her coins worth?
A carton contains milk that is 2% fat, an amount that is lower than the fat content of a carton of whole milk. What is the percentage of fat in whole milk?
$3^3 + 3^x + 3^3 =$, where x is not defined.

A long piece of paper 5 cm wide is made into a roll for cash registers by wrapping it around a cardboard tube of diameter 2 cm, forming a roll 10 cm in diameter. Approximate the length of the paper in meters. (Assume the paper forms concentric circles with diameters evenly spaced from 2 cm to 10 cm.)

A small bottle of shampoo can hold 35 milliliters of shampoo, whereas a large bottle can hold an unspecified amount of shampoo. Jasmine wants to buy the minimum number of small bottles necessary to completely fill a large bottle. How many bottles must she buy?

The figure shows a polygon formed by several congruent pieces arranged in an unspecified pattern. An extra congruent piece is attached to one of several positions along its edge. How many of the resulting figures can be folded into a cube with one face missing?
In a high school with 500 students, 40% of the seniors play a musical instrument. In all, 46.8% of the students do not play a musical instrument. How many non-seniors play a musical instrument?

In the table below, some of the entries are not provided completely. The x-values are listed as 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5, but the y-values are given only as 3, ?, 13, ?, and 31. Based on this partial information, what is the formula that relates x and y?

What is the median of the following list of numbers?
\[1, 2, 3, \ldots, k, 1^2, 2^2, 3^2, \ldots, k^2\]
The sum of all numbers of the form $2k + 1$ is:

Equilateral triangle ABP (see figure) with side AB of length 2 inches is placed inside square AXYZ with side of length 4 inches. The triangle is rotated clockwise about vertex B, then vertex P, and so on along the perimeter until a vertex returns to its starting location. What is the length of the path in inches traced by vertex P?

Jack and Jill run a race together. They start at the same point following a route over a hill and then return along that same route. Jack begins running 10 minutes before Jill and runs at speeds of 15 km/hr when climbing and 20 km/hr when descending, while Jill runs at speeds of 16 km/hr uphill and 22 km/hr downhill. How far from the top of the hill do they meet?
A straight line joins the points (-1,1) and (3,?). Its x-intercept is:
Let k be any number. The number of points common to the graphs of (x-y+2)(3x+y-4)=0 and (x+y-2)(2x-5y+k)=0 is:

At the grocery store, small boxes of facial tissue were offered with one rate last week and a different pack size rate this week. While last week the tissues were sold as 4 boxes for a given amount, this week the offer is for 5 boxes but the previous amount is not mentioned. What is the percent decrease in the price per box during the sale?
Find the sum of every integer that ends in 1.
If \(2 < \log_6 x < 3\), what is the value of \(x\)?

For each positive integer n, let a_n = \frac{(n+9)!}{(n-1)!}.
Let k denote the smallest positive integer for which the rightmost nonzero digit of a_k is odd. How?
Point F is taken in side AD of square ABCD. At C a perpendicular is drawn to CF, meeting AB extended at E. The area of triangle CEF is 200 square inches. Then the number of inches in BE is:
At the end of 1994, Walter was half as old as his grandmother. How old will Walter be at the end of 1999?

A rise of 600 feet is needed for a railroad line over a mountain. The track’s alignment is adjusted by curving it around the mountain peak so that its steepness is lessened. If the original design uses a 3% grade and the revised design adopts a gentler grade, what is the extra length of track required?
Let A be the area of the triangle with sides of length 25, 25, and x. Let B be the area of the triangle with sides of length 25, 25, and 40. What is the relationship between A and B?

Three one-inch squares are placed with their bases on a line. The center square is lifted out and rotated, as shown. Then it is centered and lowered into its original location until it touches the adjoining squares. How many inches is the point B from the line?

Two fair coins are to be tossed once. For each head that results, one fair die is rolled. What is the probability?

In the figure, it is given that angle C is nearly 90°, AD is roughly equal to DB, a line DE is drawn from D toward AB (its exact orientation is not specified), AB is approximately 20, and AC is about 12. What is the area of quadrilateral ADEC?

A ticket to a school play costs $x$ dollars, where $x$ is a whole number. One group of students buys tickets for a certain total amount, and another group of students pays a different total amount for their tickets. How many values for $x$ are possible?

On Halloween Casper ate 1/3 of his candies and then gave 2 candies to his brother. The next day he ate 1/3 of his remaining candies and then gave 4 candies to his sister. On the third day he ate his final candies. How many candies did Casper have at the beginning?
The polynomial (x+y)^9 is expanded in decreasing powers of x. The second and third terms have equal values when evaluated at x=p and y=q, where p and q are positive numbers. What is the value of p?

Let A, B and C be three distinct points on the graph of y=x^2 such that line AB is parallel to the x-axis and triangle ABC is a right triangle with area 2008. What is the sum of the digits of the y-coordinate of D?

The Incredible Hulk can alter the distance he jumps with each succeeding jump. If his first jump is 1 meter and the multiplier for each subsequent jump is not fixed, on which jump will he first jump more than 1 kilometer (1,000 meters)?
When three different numbers are multiplied, the largest possible product is

Three times Dick's age plus Tom's age is nearly twice Harry's age, though the exact difference is not specified. Also, double the cube of Harry's age is roughly equal to three times the cube of Dick's age together with the cube of Tom's age. If the three ages satisfy some coprimality conditions, what is the sum of the squares of their ages?
How many unordered pairs of edges determine a plane?
Two wheels are spun, each producing a number selected from an unspecified set. After adding the two resulting numbers, what is the probability that the sum is even?

At Euler Middle School, students voted on two issues in a school referendum with the following results: 149 voted in favor of the first issue and 119 voted in favor of the second issue. If there were exactly 29 students who voted against both issues, how many students voted in favor of both issues?

It takes Clea a certain amount of time to walk down an escalator when it is not operating, and 24 seconds when she walks down the escalator as it moves. If she chooses to just stand on the moving escalator, how many seconds does her ride take?
If 1998 is written as a product of two positive integers, then what is the difference between these two numbers?

Let S be the set of lattice points in the coordinate plane, both of whose coordinates are integers between 1 and 30, inclusive. A number of points in S lie on or below a line with equation y = mx. The possible values of m lie in an interval of length given as a fraction a/b in simplest form. What is a+b?
A box contains 11 balls, numbered 1, 2, 3, …, 11. If some balls are drawn simultaneously at random, what is the probability that the sum of the numbers on the balls drawn is odd?

The graph displays the weekly study minutes for two individuals shown with different shades. Assuming that the distribution of study minutes is not uniform across an unspecified number of days, how many additional minutes per day did the individual represented by the darker shade study compared to the one shown with the lighter shade?
If $A*B$ means $\frac{A+B}{N}$, then $(3*5)*8$ is?

At Euclid High School, the number of students taking the AMC 10 was recorded over several years with the following figures (in no particular order): 60, 66, 70, 76, 78, and 85. Assuming these recordings are not arranged by year, between what two consecutive years did the participation experience the greatest percentage jump?

Place the digits 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, and 9 into an addition problem where the positions for the digits are not fully specified. If each digit must be used exactly once, what is the smallest possible sum you can obtain from the resulting addition?
Let f(n) = A · (B)^n + C · (D)^n. Then f(n+1) - f(n-1), expressed in terms of f(n), equals:

A magazine printed photos of several celebrities along with an equal number of baby pictures that don't reveal which celebrity they belong to. Readers were challenged to match each celebrity with their respective baby picture. What is the probability that a reader guessing randomly will get every match exactly right?
If 3x^3 - 9x^2 + kx - 12 is divisible by x - 3, then it is also divisible by

A class collects an unspecified amount of money to buy flowers for a classmate who is in the hospital. Roses cost 3 dollars each, and carnations cost 2 dollars each. No other flowers are to be used. How many different bouquets could be purchased for exactly that amount?
A ream of paper is 5 cm thick. Approximately how many sheets of this type of paper would there be in a stack 7.5 cm high?
There is more than one integer greater than 1 which, when divided by an integer, gives a remainder of 1. What is the difference between the two smallest such integers?
The root(s) of \(\frac{15}{x^2 - 4}\) - \(\frac{2}{x - 2}\) is:
A number N is written in base 7. When N is expressed in base 9 the order of the digits is reversed relative to its base 7 form. What is the middle digit?

Four identical squares and one rectangle are arranged as illustrated. Without any information about how they collectively define a specific overall shape, determine the number of times the rectangle’s length exceeds its width.
In this figure \(\angle RFS = \angle FDR\), \(FD = 4\) inches, \(DR = 6\) inches, \(FR = 5\) inches. The length of \(RS\), in inches, is:
The real numbers c, b, a form an arithmetic sequence. The quadratic ax^2+bx+c has exactly one root. What?

Three generous friends, each with some money, redistribute their money in the following mysterious way: First, Amy gives just enough money to Jan and Toy so that each of their current amounts is doubled; then Jan gives an unknown amount to Amy and Toy so that their amounts are doubled again; finally, Toy gives just enough money to Amy and Jan so that their amounts are doubled at that step. If at one undisclosed stage Toy has 36 dollars and later also has 36 dollars, what is the total amount of money held collectively by the three friends—and what was the exact amount transferred in each step?

Arjun and Beth play a game in which they take turns removing bricks from a series of walls whose arrangements can change after each move. The moves they can perform alter the walls in multiple ways. For which brick arrangement is there a winning strategy for Beth?

A laser is placed at the point (3,5). The laser beam travels in a straight line. Larry wants the beam to hit and bounce off the y-axis, then hit and bounce off the x-axis, and finally hit a target point. What is the total distance the beam will travel along this path?

Placing no more than one X in each small square, what is the greatest number of X's that can be arranged on the grid shown if an X is considered to continue a line only when the difference between the indices of two adjacent squares is a negative prime number, and no three such X's may align vertically, horizontally, or diagonally?
A sphere with center O has radius 6. A triangle is situated in space so that each of its sides is tangent to the sphere. What is the distance between O and the plane determined by the triangle?

In a small pond there are eleven lily pads in a row labeled 0 through 10. A frog is on one of the lily pads. When the frog is on pad N (with 0 < N < 10), it will jump to pad N-1 with probability N/10 and to pad N+1 with probability 1-N/10. Each jump is independent of the others. If the frog reaches pad 0 it will be eaten by a waiting snake, and if it reaches pad 10 it will exit the pond. What is the probability that the frog escapes without being eaten?

Alicia had two containers. The first container was nearly full of water and the second container was empty. After she poured the water from the first container into the second, the second container ended up being roughly full. What is the ratio of the volume of the first container to the volume of the second container?

Eight points are chosen on a circle, and chords are drawn connecting every pair of points. Some of the chords might intersect at the same point inside the circle. How many triangles with all three vertices in the interior of the circle are created?

The numbers 1, 2, ..., 9 are randomly placed into the squares of a grid. Each square gets one number, and each number is used exactly once. If the grid is arranged into some rows and columns, what is the probability that the sum of the numbers in every row and every column is odd?
Karl bought five folders from Pay-A-Lot at a cost of $2.50 each. Pay-A-Lot had a sale the following day. How much could Karl have saved on the purchase by waiting a day?
The sale ad read: "Buy three tires at the regular price and get the fourth tire for 3 dollars." Sam paid 240 dollars for a set of tires at the sale. What was the regular price of one tire?
Right triangle ACD with right angle at C is constructed outwards on the hypotenuse AC of an isosceles right triangle ABC. What is sin(2∠BAD)?

How many positive integers n satisfy the following condition:
(130n)^{50} > n^{x} > 2^{200}?
(Note: The exponent x is not specified.)

Two circles lie outside a regular hexagon. One circle is tangent to one of its sides, and the other circle is tangent to a different side. In addition, each circle is tangent to two lines determined by the hexagon’s vertices. What is the ratio of the area of one circle to that of the other?
In a given plane, points A and B are chosen arbitrarily. How many positions for point C are there such that triangle ABC has a perimeter of 50 units and an area of 100 square units?
What is equivalent to \(\sqrt{\frac{x}{1-\frac{x-1}{x}}}\) when \(x < 0\) and \(x = 2\)?
In a far-off land, some fish can be exchanged for a few loaves of bread, and bread can be exchanged for several bags of rice. How many bags of rice is one fish worth?

Given a finite sequence S=(a_1,a_2,\ldots ,a_n) of n real numbers, let A(S) be the sequence \(\left(\frac{a_1+a_2}{2},\frac{a_2+a_3}{2},\ldots ,\frac{a_{n-1}+a_n}{2}\right)\) of n-1 real numbers. Define A^1(S)=A(S) and, for each integer m, 2\le m\le n-1, define A^m(S)=A(A^{m-1}(S)). Suppose x>0, and let S=(1,x,x^2,\ldots ,x^{100}). If A^{101}(S)=\left(\frac{1}{2^{50}}\right), then what is x?
Inside a right circular cone are three congruent spheres that are tangent to each other as well as to the cone’s base and lateral side. What is the radius of each sphere?
A circle is inscribed in a triangle with side lengths 8, 13, and 17. Let the segments formed by a point of tangency on one of the sides be r and s, with r<s. What is the ratio r:s?

An arithmetic sequence is a sequence in which each term after the first is obtained by adding a constant to the previous term. For example, 2,5,8,11,14 is an arithmetic sequence. Consider a 5×5 array where every row and every column is claimed to be an arithmetic sequence, though no details are given about their starting values or common differences. The center square is marked X. Without any additional conditions on how the sequences are chosen, what is the value of X?
Square EFGH is inside the square ABCD so that each side of EFGH can be extended to pass through a vertex of ABCD. Square ABCD has side length √50. What is the area of the inner square EFGH?

When Ringo places his marbles into evenly filled bags, he ends up with 4 marbles remaining. Meanwhile, Paul does the same with his marbles and ends up with 3 marbles extra. If they combine their marbles and then distribute them into as many complete bags as possible, what is the number of marbles that remain?
